scores.reasoning: The homepage text describes Asana as a work and project management platform for teams, tracking tasks, projects, and workflows, consistent with the record's description.
The authentication page explicitly documents OAuth 2.0 and personal access tokens (PATs) used as Bearer tokens, matching the recorded auth_methods value and note.

The personal access token page confirms PATs can be created from the developer console without human approval and that OAuth client credentials are created similarly; no paid plan requirement for API access is stated.
The cited API reference URL returns a 404 page not found error, so the evidence does not support the claim of a broad REST API surface documented at that URL.

The combination of authentication docs and API reference (despite the 404 on the API reference page) plus the publicly accessible documentation supports that integrations can be built today without blockers.
